Name: GISA State Championship (Varsity)
Location: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2019 GISA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The varsity file showed three St. Andrew's girls individual state titles from Gaby Van Brunt, two scoring girls relays, a fourth-place girls team finish with 43 points, and a seventh-place boys medley relay that produced the program's four boys team points.
RecapTitle: Van Brunt's three titles launch the loaded 2019 archive
RecapParagraphs: Gaby Van Brunt headlined St. Andrew's at the 2019 GISA State Championship by winning the girls 50 freestyle in a record 23.75, the 100 freestyle in 52.40, and the 100 backstroke in a record 58.20.

The girls also scored in both varsity relays. Marie Audebert, Libby Peaster, Van Brunt, and Molly Fox-Marrs finished fifth in the 200 medley relay, and Fox-Marrs, Audebert, Sarah Rowland, and Van Brunt took fifth in the 200 freestyle relay.

Those relay points helped St. Andrew's finish fourth in the girls varsity standings with 43 points, while Tyler Awad, Sumter Archer, Noah Linfoot, and Matt Eling added a seventh-place boys 200 medley relay to put the Lions 12th on the boys side.

Name: GISA State Championship (Middle School)
Location: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2019 GISA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The middle-school file showed sprint, backstroke, and breaststroke entries from Luke Peaster and Ian Given, but no St. Andrew's middle-school team points.
RecapTitle: Peaster and Given carry the 2019 middle school entries
RecapParagraphs: St. Andrew's middle-school results in 2019 came entirely from Luke Peaster and Ian Given on the boys side.
Peaster swam the 50 freestyle, 50 backstroke, and 50 breaststroke, while Given matched that same three-event slate at the state meet.
Neither swimmer reached the team scoring range, but the archive now preserves the full middle-school St. Andrew's presence from January 26, 2019.

Name: GISA State Championship (Varsity)
Location: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2020 GISA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The varsity file showed two St. Andrew's girls individual state titles from Gaby Van Brunt, a third-place girls team finish with 47 points, and full boys varsity relay and individual entries.
RecapTitle: Third-place girls finish powers the loaded 2020 varsity meet
RecapParagraphs: St. Andrew's rose to third in the girls varsity standings at the 2020 GISA State Championship behind another big day from Gaby Van Brunt. Van Brunt placed second in the 50 freestyle, then won the 100 butterfly in 57.05 and the 100 freestyle in 54.06.

Addy Sy added the rest of the individual varsity girls scoring by placing seventh in the 200 IM and fifth in the 100 backstroke. The girls also scored in both relays, with Marie Audebert, Sy, Van Brunt, and Molly Fox-Marrs finishing fifth in the 200 medley relay and Sy, Fox-Marrs, Sarah Rowland, and Van Brunt taking fifth in the 200 freestyle relay.

The boys varsity group did not score as a team, but St. Andrew's still logged full relay entries plus sprint and stroke swims from Matt Eling, Noah Linfoot, Will Ashton, Tyler Awad, and Sumter Archer.

Name: GISA State Championship (Middle School)
Location: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2020 GISA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The middle-school file showed Ian Given's entries across three boys events and Sierra Abbasi's girls entries, with Given's eighth-place 50 breaststroke earning the lone St. Andrew's team point and a 15th-place boys finish.
RecapTitle: Given scores the lone 2020 middle school team point
RecapParagraphs: Ian Given and Sierra Abbasi carried the St. Andrew's middle-school results at the 2020 state meet.
Given swam the boys 50 freestyle, 50 backstroke, and 50 breaststroke, finishing eighth in the breaststroke to account for the team's lone point and a 15th-place boys finish.
Abbasi represented the girls in the 50 butterfly, 100 freestyle, and 50 breaststroke, giving the archive a full picture of the younger St. Andrew's group from January 25, 2020.

Name: GISA State Championship (Varsity)
Location: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2022 GISA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The varsity file showed three St. Andrew's girls individual state titles from Gaby Van Brunt, a sixth-place girls finish with 27 points, and strong boys scoring from Caden Cahill and Ian Given that produced a seventh-place boys finish with 20 points.
RecapTitle: Van Brunt sweeps three more titles in the 2022 varsity meet
RecapParagraphs: Gaby Van Brunt again dominated the girls state meet for St. Andrew's, winning the 50 freestyle in a record 22.99, the 100 butterfly in a record 54.13, and the 100 backstroke in a record 54.17.

Those three wins accounted for all 27 girls points and pushed the Lions to sixth in the varsity standings. Caden Cahill powered the boys side with third-place finishes in the 200 freestyle, 100 butterfly, and 100 backstroke.
Ian Given added seventh-place points in the 200 IM, giving St. Andrew's 20 boys points and a seventh-place team finish.

Name: GISA State Championship (Middle School)
Location: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2022 GISA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The middle-school file showed Sydney Linfoot on the girls side and Austin Waters, Robert Federal, and Eli Rall on the boys side across sprint, butterfly, backstroke, and breaststroke events, but no St. Andrew's team scoring.
RecapTitle: A small 2022 middle school group extends the archive
RecapParagraphs: Sydney Linfoot gave St. Andrew's a girls middle-school entry in the 50 freestyle at the 2022 state meet.
Austin Waters and Robert Federal added boys sprint and breaststroke swims, while Eli Rall appeared in the boys 50 freestyle, 50 butterfly, and 50 backstroke.
The younger Lions did not place into the team-scoring range, but their entries now round out the program's 2022 state-meet archive.

Name: GIAA State Championship (Varsity)
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2023 GIAA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The varsity file showed three St. Andrew's girls individual state titles from Gaby Van Brunt, a seventh-place girls team finish with 27 points, a ninth-place boys finish with nine points, and a ninth-place combined team finish with 36 points.
RecapTitle: Van Brunt's triple-title day anchors the first loaded 2023 season
RecapParagraphs: Gaby Van Brunt gave St. Andrew's one of the strongest single-meet performances in the swim archive by winning the girls 100 butterfly in a record 53.03, the girls 100 freestyle in 51.10, and the girls 100 backstroke in 54.57.

Those three titles produced all 27 girls points and carried the Lions to seventh in the varsity girls standings. Ian Given supplied the boys scoring by finishing seventh in the 100 freestyle, fourth in the 100 backstroke, and seventh in the 100 breaststroke.
With Given's nine points added to Van Brunt's 27, St. Andrew's finished ninth in the combined varsity standings with 36 points.

Name: GIAA State Championship (Middle School)
Notes: Results loaded from the official 2023 GIAA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The middle-school file showed scoring freestyle swims from Tristan Tsang, a ninth-place boys 200 freestyle relay, a completed boys 200 medley relay, and an overall St. Andrew's combined middle-school finish of 20th with 11 points.
RecapTitle: Middle school freestyle points and relay depth extend the archive
RecapParagraphs: The middle-school St. Andrew's group contributed the rest of the program's 2023 state-meet archive. Tristan Tsang placed fourth in the boys 50 freestyle and third in the boys 100 freestyle to account for the team's scoring points.

The boys 200 freestyle relay of Tristan Tsang, Eli Rall, Oliver Kovel, and Robert Federal IV finished ninth in 2:13.28, and the boys 200 medley relay of Miles Glienke, Robert Federal IV, Eli Rall, and Oliver Kovel also completed the meet.

The middle-school archive also preserves additional St. Andrew's entries across girls freestyle, backstroke, and breaststroke plus several disqualifications and no-starts, helping the combined middle-school team finish 20th overall with 11 points.

Name: GIAA State Championship (Varsity)
Location: McAuley Aquatic Center, Georgia Tech, Atlanta, GA
Notes: Results loaded from the official GIAA state-meet PDF shared by the user. The varsity file showed three St. Andrew's girls individual state titles from Jossy Thompson, a seventh-place girls team finish with 27 points, and a sixth-place boys team finish with 28 points.
RecapTitle: Triple-title state meet opens the swim archive
RecapParagraphs: The first loaded St. Andrew's swim meet already reads like a breakthrough day. Jossy Thompson won the girls 50 freestyle in 24.45, the girls 100 butterfly in 58.33, and the girls 100 breaststroke in 1:08.15 to give the Lions three varsity individual state championships in one afternoon.

The boys meet was built on balance. Ian Given scored in three events by taking third in the 200 IM, seventh in the 100 freestyle, and fourth in the 100 backstroke, while Tristan Tsang reached the podium in both distance and butterfly with a fifth-place 500 freestyle and an eighth-place 100 butterfly.

St. Andrew's also scored in both varsity boys relays. The 200 medley relay of James Crake, Ian Given, Tristan Tsang, and Ian Willis finished sixth, and the 200 freestyle relay of Tate Salter, Ian Willis, Ian Given, and Tristan Tsang finished seventh, helping the boys team close the meet in sixth place overall.
